quick_facts.label: Role pattern
quick_facts.value: In India, “Research Assistant” is not a single standardized occupation: job requirements and duties are set by the sponsoring project, discipline and employer. Official ICMR and university advertisements show roles spanning health, social-science, data and technology projects.

quick_facts.label: Typical appointment status
quick_facts.value: Many university and government-funded project Research Assistant posts are temporary, performance-linked and co-terminus with the project rather than permanent institutional posts.

quick_facts.label: Education threshold
quick_facts.value: Eligibility varies substantially: an ICMR Research Assistant advertisement accepted a relevant master’s degree or a relevant bachelor’s degree plus three years’ experience, while ICSSR-funded university posts commonly sought a relevant postgraduate degree with 55% marks plus NET/M.Phil./Ph.D. credentials.

quick_facts.label: Pay context, not a national salary scale
quick_facts.value: Published project advertisements illustrate variable consolidated pay: ₹31,000 per month in one ICMR Research Assistant advertisement and ₹37,000 per month in several 2025–26 ICSSR-funded university advertisements. These are dated examples, not a universal salary entitlement.

sections.heading: What a Research Assistant does
paragraphs: Research Assistants support the principal investigator and project team in activities that depend on the study. In official ICMR examples, this includes compiling and collating multi-site data, conducting site visits, coordinating project activities, and contributing to scientific reports and manuscripts. Other health-research advertisements specify quantitative and qualitative data analysis, report writing and manuscript writing.

For laboratory, biomedical, engineering and data-oriented projects, the work may additionally require discipline-specific experimental, computational or analytical activity. Applicants should therefore read the project title, essential qualifications and stated duties—not the job title alone—to determine whether a vacancy is lab-based, field-based, desk-based or mixed.
bullets: Collect, clean, collate and analyse research data.
Assist with project coordination, monitoring, meetings or site visits where required.
Prepare reports, documentation and, in some posts, manuscripts for publication.
Carry out project-specific tasks assigned by the principal investigator, which may include fieldwork, software-supported analysis or research-platform support.

sections.heading: Entry routes from the listed parent qualifications
paragraphs: A relevant bachelor’s degree can be a route into Research Assistant work when paired with relevant experience. For example, an ICMR project advertisement accepted graduates in relevant subjects with three years’ experience, or candidates with a relevant master’s degree. This makes laboratory technician experience and project exposure potentially useful bridges for candidates from science, biotechnology, food technology, veterinary, medical-science and allied-health backgrounds.

For social-science, public-policy, law and humanities projects, university advertisements commonly specify a relevant postgraduate degree with at least 55% marks and often ask for NET/SLET, M.Phil. or Ph.D. qualifications. Accordingly, MA, LL.M. and MPH graduates should target vacancies whose discipline, methods and subject area match their training.

Technical and biomedical routes are also project-specific. A JNU biotechnology project accepted M.Sc., M.Tech., or B.Tech. candidates with two years’ experience and required prior work with biological data, analytical tools and deep-learning methods. Candidates should build evidence of the exact methods named in advertisements rather than assume that any degree alone qualifies them.
bullets: Apply to project vacancies aligned with the subject area of the degree and any practical research experience.
Use internships, dissertations, lab technician work, field surveys, data projects or research internships to demonstrate relevant experience.
For posts that state NET, M.Phil. or Ph.D. as eligibility or preference, obtain the stated credential before applying or target positions with alternative eligibility.
Submit a tailored CV and the degree, marksheet and experience evidence requested in the advertisement; selection is commonly through shortlisting and interview.

sections.heading: Skills employers commonly seek
paragraphs: The strongest recurring skills in official advertisements are research-data skills and clear communication: data collection and handling, statistical or qualitative analysis, report writing, and written and verbal communication. ICMR advertisements also list MS Office and statistical-tool knowledge; an IIT Kanpur project advertisement named SPSS, R, Python, MATLAB and Mathematica as relevant tools.

The technical toolkit should follow the research area. For example, health and social-research roles may value fieldwork, survey or multi-site-study experience, while computational biology roles can require biological-data analysis and deep-learning frameworks. Build a portfolio that documents methods used, data handled, reports written and any reproducible analysis or laboratory protocols completed, while respecting confidentiality and research ethics.
bullets: Research methods: data collection, fieldwork or laboratory/project procedures appropriate to the discipline.
Analysis: quantitative, qualitative or mixed-methods analysis; statistical software where required.
Documentation: accurate data handling, report writing and manuscript support.
Professional skills: English communication, teamwork, project coordination and the ability to follow a principal investigator’s research protocol.
Technical tools: MS Office plus domain-specific tools such as SPSS, R, Python, MATLAB or deep-learning frameworks when the vacancy specifies them.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: Research Assistants work in universities, government research bodies, institutes and sponsored research projects. The workplace can be a campus department, research headquarters, laboratory or data-focused office; it can also involve field sites and travel. One ICMR advertisement placed the role at its New Delhi headquarters but required site visits and project work across India, including remote areas.

Work arrangements are driven by the study design. A data or AI project may be largely analytical, whereas public-health and social-science projects may require community engagement, surveys, workshops, multi-site coordination or archival research. Confirm the place of posting, travel expectations, duration and whether the role is project-co-terminus before accepting an offer.
bullets: Academic departments and university-funded research projects.
Government and autonomous research institutions, including medical and public-health research bodies.
Laboratory, data-analysis, field-research or hybrid settings.
Temporary sponsored projects with a defined duration and specified posting location.

sections.heading: Progression and next steps
paragraphs: Research Assistant experience can build the methods, project-delivery record and research outputs used to pursue more advanced research roles or doctoral study, but progression is not automatic because many appointments end with the project. A current JNU project advertisement illustrates a distinct Research Associate level requiring a Ph.D. and prior research experience, alongside a Research Assistant position paid at a lower consolidated rate.

Practical progression is therefore portfolio-based: seek progressively complex responsibilities, document analytical and writing contributions, and pursue the higher qualification or specialized methods requested in target roles. Candidates seeking a stable career should also monitor permanent recruitment separately; a project Research Assistant appointment should not be assumed to convert into a permanent university or government post.
bullets: Strengthen research experience through data analysis, field/lab methods, reports and publications where permitted.
Pursue a relevant master’s, NET or doctoral pathway when target advertisements require it.
Progress to higher project roles only when the required qualification and experience are met; titles, duties and pay differ by employer.
Treat each new project appointment as a separate recruitment opportunity unless an advertisement expressly states otherwise.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide pay figure for the Research Assistant title. As dated illustrations, an ICMR Headquarters project advertisement listed ₹31,000 per month consolidated for a Project Assistant (Research Assistant)/Project Technical Support III role, while an IIT Kanpur ICSSR project advertisement dated May 2026 listed ₹37,000 per month consolidated. A JNU biotechnology project instead listed ₹20,000 plus HRA for a temporary Research Assistant role.

Compensation must be assessed vacancy by vacancy. Compare the consolidated amount, HRA or other stated allowance, contract duration, location, travel/fieldwork expectations, eligibility level and whether the appointment is co-terminus with the funded project. Do not infer a universal salary or benefit package from these examples.
bullets: Published pay varies by funder, discipline, qualification requirements, location and project budget.
Advertisements may specify consolidated pay, HRA, or both; benefits should not be presumed if they are not stated.
Short project duration and temporary status are material parts of total compensation context.
